seoTitle: Wilby vs Wildwood — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Wilby and Wildwood. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Wilby and Wildwood.
heading: Wilby vs Wildwood — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Wilby, Victoria and Wildwood, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wildwood has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Wilby (48/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Wilby or Wildwood safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Wilby has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Wildwood scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Wilby scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Wildwood scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Wilby or Wildwood?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wildwood has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
